task: I need to send a short update to a client about a paperwork delay, and I want to make sure it covers the important points without missing anything I should mention. Can you help me put together a draft I can send today?

prefs.explanation: He values practicality and clear communication, so he likely wants answers that get to the point without extra filler. His work habits suggest he appreciates efficiency over extended discussion. He may still accept some detail when it helps avoid mistakes, but not long-winded explanations.

He keeps glossaries, checklists, and paperwork, which suggests he responds well to structured output. A clean format would fit his preference for reliability and easy reference. He probably dislikes rambling prose when a simple list or step-by-step layout would do.

His style appears grounded in clarity and avoiding ambiguity, especially when wording matters. He likely wants the assistant to say what it means plainly rather than hedge excessively. Overly soft or evasive phrasing would probably feel unhelpful to him.

His life attitude is quiet and cautious rather than emotionally expressive, so a steady tone fits best. He likely responds well to calm professionalism more than enthusiasm or hype. He may appreciate warmth, but only in a restrained, respectful way.

prefs.short: Wants enough detail to act, not exhaustive digressions
prefs.explanation: He seems to prefer information that is usable and concrete, especially when deadlines or paperwork are involved. Too much background would likely slow him down, but too little detail risks errors. He probably wants the main point plus any important caveats, not a full lecture.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short draft you can send today:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: there’s a delay with the paperwork due to [brief reason]. I’m working to get it resolved and expect to have it to you by [new date/time].

Nothing should change on your end for now, but I’ll let you know right away if anything does.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also make this a little more polished or more casual.

per_pref.reason: Very concise and no-fluff: "here’s a short draft you can send today" and the draft itself is brief and practical.
Well organized as a ready-to-use template with clear lines and placeholders, though not explicitly checklist-style.
Straightforward and plainly stated: "there’s a delay," "I’m working to get it resolved," and "Nothing should change on your end for now."
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout; no hype or emotional language, just a professional update.
Provides enough detail to act: mentions the delay, brief reason placeholder, expected new date/time, and what the client should expect next.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version that covers those points:

Hi [Client Name],

Quick update: the paperwork is delayed on my side because I’m still waiting on [one item / one piece of information] from [my team / you / the other party]. I already have everything else I need, and the timeline has shifted to [new date/time].

Nothing else should change for you right now, but I’ll keep you posted as soon as I have it.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can also give you:
- a version that sounds a little more polished
- a version that’s more direct/plain
- one with “your side” vs “my side” already filled in

per_pref.reason: Very concise and practical: "here’s a tighter version" and the draft is short, with no extra filler.
Well organized in a clean template format with placeholders and a short bullet list of alternatives, though not a full checklist.
Straightforward and explicit: "the paperwork is delayed on my side because..." and "the timeline has shifted to..." with little hedging.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout, e.g. "Quick update" and "Nothing else should change for you right now," without hype or emotional language.
Provides enough detail to act: cause of delay, what’s already done, new timeline, and reassurance about impact, without digressing.
